devanagari: अथ त्रिंशोऽध्यायः । श्रीराजोवाच । ततो महाभागवत उद्धवे निर्गते वनम् । द्वारवत्यां किमकरोद्भगवान्भूतभावनः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha triṃśo śrīrājovāca tato mahābhāgavata uddhave nirgate vanam dvāravatyāṃ kimakarodbhagavānbhūtabhāvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) enquired:
When Uddhava, a great votary of the Lord, repaired to the forest (at Badarikāśrama), what did the glorious Lord, the creator of all beings, do at Dvārakā?

devanagari: ब्रह्मशापोपसंसृष्टे स्वकुले यादवर्षभः । प्रेयसीं सर्वनेत्राणां तनुं स कथमत्यजत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaśāpopasaṃsṛṣṭe svakule yādavarṣabhaḥ preyasīṃ sarvanetrāṇāṃ tanuṃ sa kathamatyajat
translation: When his own race was threatened with annihilation by the curse of Brāhmaṇas, how did the Lord of Yādavas cast off his body which was beloved-most to the eyes of all. His personality, so charming that women, having but once looked at it, could not take away their eyes from it; which having once entered into the ears of the saintly persons gets (as if) inscribed in their heart and never leaves it (i.e. is never forgotten by them,): Whose glorious splendour when extolled by poets, brings special charms to their words and enhances respect for them; having seen that form occupying the (charioteer’s) seat in Arjuna’s chariot, the warriors who were slain in the (battle at Kurukṣetra) in his presence, attained a form similar to him.

devanagari: प्रत्याक्रष्टुं नयनमबला यत्र लग्नं न शेकुः । कर्णाविष्टं न सरति ततो यत्सतामात्मलग्नम् । यच्छ्रीर्वाचां जनयति रतिं किं नु मानं कवीनां । ...
english_devnagari: pratyākraṣṭuṃ nayanamabalā yatra lagnaṃ na śekuḥ karṇāviṣṭaṃ na sarati tato yatsatāmātmalagnam yacchrīrvācāṃ janayati ratiṃ kiṃ nu mānaṃ kavīnāṃ dṛṣṭvā jiṣṇoryudhi rathagataṃ yacca tatsāmyamīyuḥ

translation: When his own race was threatened with annihilation by the curse of Brāhmaṇas, how did the Lord of Yādavas cast off his body which was beloved-most to the eyes of all. His personality, so charming that women, having but once looked at it, could not take away their eyes from it; which having once entered into the ears of the saintly persons gets (as if) inscribed in their heart and never leaves it (i.e. is never forgotten by them,): Whose glorious splendour when extolled by poets, brings special charms to their words and enhances respect for them; having seen that form occupying the (charioteer’s) seat in Arjuna’s chariot, the warriors who were slain in the (battle at Kurukṣetra) in his presence, attained a form similar to him.

devanagari: श्री ऋषिरुवाच । दिवि भुव्यन्तरिक्षे च महोत्पातान्समुत्थितान् । दृष्ट्वासीनान्सुधर्मायां कृष्णः प्राह यदूनिदम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrī ṛṣiruvāca divi bhuvyantarikṣe ca mahotpātānsamutthitān dṛṣṭvāsīnānsudharmāyāṃ kṛṣṇaḥ prāha yadūnidam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The sage Śrī Śuka replied:
Noticing terrible portents appearing in the sky, on the earth and in the mid-air, Kṛṣṇa addressed the Yādavas who were seated in the assembly hall called ‘Sudharmā’, as follows:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एते घोरा महोत्पाता द्वार्वत्यां यमकेतवः । मुहूर्तमपि न स्थेयमत्र नो यदुपुङ्गवाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca ete ghorā mahotpātā dvārvatyāṃ yamaketavaḥ muhūrtamapi na stheyamatra no yadupuṅgavāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: “O foremost of Yādavas! These terrible portents of a serious nature like unto the flags of the god of Death (Yama) are appearing in Dvārakā. We should not tarry here even for a short while.

devanagari: स्त्रियो बालाश्च वृद्धाश्च शङ्खोद्धारं व्रजन्त्वितः । वयं प्रभासं यास्यामो यत्र प्रत्यक्सरस्वती ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: striyo bālāśca vṛddhāśca śaṅkhoddhāraṃ vrajantvitaḥ vayaṃ prabhāsaṃ yāsyāmo yatra pratyaksarasvatī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: Let women, children and old people go from this place to Śaṅkhoddhāra[1]. We shall go to the holy place Prabhāsa[2] where the Sarasvatī[3] flows to the west.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The island of Bati (Beyt) at the north-western extremity of the gulf of Cutch. Viṣṇu is said to have killed Śaṅkhāsura and recovered the Vedas from him here. GDAMI, P. 177.

Footnote [2]:
Somanath or Devapaṭṭana near Verawal in Saurashtra.

Footnote [3]:
Now called Raunākṣī. On the bank of this river under an aspen tree, Kṛṣṇa passed away—GDAMI, P. 181.

devanagari: तत्राभिषिच्य शुचय उपोष्य सुसमाहिताः । देवताः पूजयिष्यामः स्नपनालेपनार्हणैः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrābhiṣicya śucaya upoṣya susamāhitāḥ devatāḥ pūjayiṣyāmaḥ snapanālepanārhaṇaiḥ
translation: We shall take our bath there, observe a fast and serenely meditate. We shall also worship our deities with ablutions and application of sandal-paste and offering other articles of worship.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणांस्तु महाभागान्कृतस्वस्त्ययना वयम् । गोभूहिरण्यवासोभिर्गजाश्वरथवेश्मभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇāṃstu mahābhāgānkṛtasvastyayanā vayam gobhūhiraṇyavāsobhirgajāśvarathaveśmabhiḥ
translation: Having received the benediction (of Brāhmaṇas) by the recitation of sacred Mantras and performance of expiatory rites, we shall worship them with gifts of cows, land, gold, cloths, elephants, horses, chariots and houses.

devanagari: विधिरेष ह्यरिष्टघ्नो मङ्गलायनमुत्तमम् । देवद्विजगवां पूजा भूतेषु परमो भवः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vidhireṣa hyariṣṭaghno maṅgalāyanamuttamam devadvijagavāṃ pūjā bhūteṣu paramo bhavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: For this is the surest procedure to avert evil and the best means of ensuring auspiciousness. Worship of gods, Brāhmaṇas and cows definitely brings prosperity to beings both here and hereafter.”

devanagari: इति सर्वे समाकर्ण्य यदुवृद्धा मधुद्विषः । तथेति नौभिरुत्तीर्य प्रभासं प्रययू रथैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti sarve samākarṇya yaduvṛddhā madhudviṣaḥ tatheti naubhiruttīrya prabhāsaṃ prayayū rathaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: All the (council of) elder Yadus attentively listened to this advice of Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of demon Madhu. They agreed with his proposal, ferried across the sea in boats and drove to Prabhāsa in their chariots.

devanagari: तस्मिन्भगवतादिष्टं यदुदेवेन यादवाः । चक्रुः परमया भक्त्या सर्वश्रेयोपबृंहितम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminbhagavatādiṣṭaṃ yadudevena yādavāḥ cakruḥ paramayā bhaktyā sarvaśreyopabṛṃhitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śrā class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śrā class 4 verb]

[vocative single from √ śrai class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śrai class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śrai class 4 verb]

[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: At Prabhāsa, as per instructions of the Lord, whom they regarded as their deity, the Yādavas performed with great faith and devotion, all the rites conducive to their prosperity.

devanagari: ततस्तस्मिन्महापानं पपुर्मैरेयकं मधु । दिष्टविभ्रंशितधियो यद्द्रवैर्भ्रश्यते मतिः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastasminmahāpānaṃ papurmaireyakaṃ madhu diṣṭavibhraṃśitadhiyo yaddravairbhraśyate matiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: At that very sacred place, as Fate would have in Yādavas lost their judgement and drank the highly intoxicating sweet beverage, a liquor called Maireyaka[4] by the filtrates of which the reasoning faculty is completely lost.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
SKD III., P. 786 gives the following ingredients of this wine:
sīdhur ikṣurasaiḥ pakvair apakvair āsavo bhavet /
maireyaṃ dhātakī-puṣpaṃ guḍa-dhānānnasaṃhitam //
I could not trace this verse in Mādhava-nidāna if by ‘Mādhava’ that work is indicated in SKD. From its name it (the liquor) appears to be a stimulant.

devanagari: महापानाभिमत्तानां वीराणां दृप्तचेतसाम् । कृष्णमायाविमूढानां सङ्घर्षः सुमहानभूत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: mahāpānābhimattānāṃ vīrāṇāṃ dṛptacetasām kṛṣṇamāyāvimūḍhānāṃ saṅgharṣaḥ sumahānabhūt
translation: There broke out a bitter conflict between the proud, and haughty warriors who were dead drunk with a strong liquor and were deluded by the Māyā of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: युयुधुः क्रोधसंरब्धा वेलायामाततायिनः । धनुर्भिरसिभिर्भल्लैर्गदाभिस्तोमरर्ष्टिभिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yuyudhuḥ krodhasaṃrabdhā velāyāmātatāyinaḥ dhanurbhirasibhirbhallairgadābhistomararṣṭibhiḥ
translation: Infuriated with rage[5], those desperadoes fought each other with bows, swords, spears, maces, iron clubs, and lances on the sea-shore.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
The Mahābhārata describes how Yuyudhāna’s criticism of Krtavarmā’s dastardly attack on the sleeping Pāṇḍava army, sparked off the struggle The whole report (Mahābhārata Mausal 3.1766) reads like an account by an eye-witness

devanagari: पतत्पताकै रथकुञ्जरादिभिः खरोष्ट्रगोभिर्महिषैर्नरैरपि । मिथः समेत्याश्वतरैः सुदुर्मदा न्यहन्शरैर्दद्भिरिव द्विपा वने ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: patatpatākai rathakuñjarādibhiḥ kharoṣṭragobhirmahiṣairnarairapi mithaḥ sametyāśvataraiḥ sudurmadā nyahanśarairdadbhiriva dvipā vane

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Confronting each other in their chariots, elephants etc. decked with banners flowing in the air, and riding on donkeys, camels, bulls, buffaloes, mules and human beings (slaves), the highly intoxicated Yādava warriors struck one another with arrows even as wild elephants dash against each other with their tusks.

devanagari: प्रद्युम्नसाम्बौ युधि रूढमत्सराव् । अक्रूरभोजावनिरुद्धसात्यकी । सुभद्र सङ्ग्रामजितौ सुदारुणौ । गदौ सुमित्रासुरथौ समीयतुः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: pradyumnasāmbau yudhi rūḍhamatsarāv akrūrabhojāvaniruddhasātyakī subhadra saṅgrāmajitau sudāruṇau gadau sumitrāsurathau samīyatuḥ
translation: With increasing intense hostility while fighting, there were duels between Pradyumna and Sāmba, Akrūra and Bhoja, Aniruddha and Sātyaki, Subhadra and Saṅgrāmajit, terrible (uncle and cousin) of the (same) name Gada (one being the brother and the other a son of Kṛṣṇa), Sumitra and Suratha as combatants.

devanagari: अन्ये च ये वै निशठोल्मुकादयः सहस्रजिच्छतजिद्भानुमुख्याः । अन्योन्यमासाद्य मदान्धकारिता जघ्नुर्मुकुन्देन विमोहिता भृशम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: anye ca ye vai niśaṭholmukādayaḥ sahasrajicchatajidbhānumukhyāḥ anyonyamāsādya madāndhakāritā jaghnurmukundena vimohitā bhṛśam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ kṛ ], [vocative plural from √ kṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ kṛ ], [vocative plural from √ kṛ ], [accusative plural from √ kṛ ]

translation: While others like Niśatha, Ulmuka and others the chief of whom were Sahasrajit, Śatajit and Bhānu became blind with intoxication and deluded by (the Māyā of) Lord Kṛṣṇa, challenged each other and meeting at close quarters struck each other mercilessly.

devanagari: दाशार्हवृष्ण्यन्धकभोजसात्वता । मध्वर्बुदा माथुरशूरसेनाः । विसर्जनाः कुकुराः कुन्तयश्च । मिथस्तु जघ्नुः सुविसृज्य सौहृदम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dāśārhavṛṣṇyandhakabhojasātvatā madhvarbudā māthuraśūrasenāḥ visarjanāḥ kukurāḥ kuntayaśca mithastu jaghnuḥ suvisṛjya sauhṛdam
translation: Forgetting their mutual friendly relations, all the clans of Yādavas such as Dāśārhas, Vṛṣṇa, Andhakas, Bhojas, Sātvatas, Madhus, Arbudas, Māthura and Śūrasenas, the Visarjanas, the Kukuras and the Kuntis (out of them some were domiciled in countries like Arbuda, Mathura, and Śūrasena) fought with one another.

devanagari: पुत्रा अयुध्यन्पितृभिर्भ्रातृभिश्च । स्वस्रीयदौहित्रपितृव्यमातुलैः । मित्राणि मित्रैः सुहृदः सुहृद्भिर् । ज्ञातींस्त्वहन्ज्ञातय एव मूढ...
english_devnagari: putrā ayudhyanpitṛbhirbhrātṛbhiśca svasrīyadauhitrapitṛvyamātulaiḥ mitrāṇi mitraiḥ suhṛdaḥ suhṛdbhir jñātīṃstvahanjñātaya eva mūḍhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]

translation: Sons fought with fathers, brothers with brothers, maternal uncles with their sister’s sons, nephews with uncles, friends with friends, well-wishers with well-wishers and clansmen with clansmen, as all of them were stupefied and deluded (by Hari’s Māyā).

devanagari: शरेषु क्षीयमाणेषु भज्यमानेसु धन्वसु । शस्त्रेषु क्षीयमानेषु मुष्टिभिर्जह्रुरेरकाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śareṣu kṣīyamāṇeṣu bhajyamānesu dhanvasu śastreṣu kṣīyamāneṣu muṣṭibhirjahrurerakāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative plural from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 9 verb]
[locative plural from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 9 verb]
[locative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [locative plural from √ man class 8 verb]
[locative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [locative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: (After a time) when the (stocks of) arrows was being exhausted and bows (which they used like sticks) got broken and their weaponry was finished, they (pulled out and) clenched into fists the Erakā grass (that grew abundantly on the shore) as weapons.

devanagari: ता वज्रकल्पा ह्यभवन्परिघा मुष्टिना भृताः । जघ्नुर्द्विषस्तैः कृष्णेन वार्यमाणास्तु तं च ते ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tā vajrakalpā hyabhavanparighā muṣṭinā bhṛtāḥ jaghnurdviṣastaiḥ kṛṣṇena vāryamāṇāstu taṃ ca te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vṛ ], [vocative plural from √ vṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ vṛ ], [vocative plural from √ vṛ ], [accusative plural from √ vṛ ]

translation: And lo! The Erakā grass when held by them in their fists, became adamant like hard iron-bars with which they struck their adversaries. When forbidden by Kṛṣṇa, they (turned against him and) attacked him.

devanagari: प्रत्यनीकं मन्यमाना बलभद्रं च मोहिताः । हन्तुं कृतधियो राजन्नापन्ना आततायिनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyanīkaṃ manyamānā balabhadraṃ ca mohitāḥ hantuṃ kṛtadhiyo rājannāpannā ātatāyinaḥ

translation: O king! Being infatuated they looked upon (kṛṣṇa) and Balarāma as their enemies. Those desperadoes determined to kill them and attacked them, O king.

devanagari: अथ तावपि सङ्क्रुद्धावुद्यम्य कुरुनन्दन । एरकामुष्टिपरिघौ चरन्तौ जघ्नतुर्युधि ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tāvapi saṅkruddhāvudyamya kurunandana erakāmuṣṭiparighau carantau jaghnaturyudhi
translation: At this, they (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) on their part became enraged, O Parīkṣit (scion of the Kuru race). They uprooted a handful of Erakā grass which (automatically was transformed into iron bars wherewith they struck back and moved about in the battle, killing (Yādavas with their iron clubs).

devanagari: ब्रह्मशापोपसृष्टानां कृष्णमायावृतात्मनाम् । स्पर्धाक्रोधः क्षयं निन्ये वैणवोऽग्निर्यथा वनम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaśāpopasṛṣṭānāṃ kṛṣṇamāyāvṛtātmanām spardhākrodhaḥ kṣayaṃ ninye vaiṇavo

translation: The wrath intensified with jealousy of Yādavas who were bedevilled by Brahmanical curse and whose minds were completely perverted by Kṛṣṇa’s deluding potency which wrought their complete annihilation even as fire sparked off by friction of bamboos, reduces the whole forest to ashes.

devanagari: एवं नष्टेषु सर्वेषु कुलेषु स्वेषु केशवः । अवतारितो भुवो भार इति मेनेऽवशेषितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ naṣṭeṣu sarveṣu kuleṣu sveṣu keśavaḥ avatārito bhuvo bhāra iti mene

translation: When all clansmen belonging to his race were thus done to death, Lord Kṛṣṇa felt that all the remnant of the burden of the earth had been lightened.

devanagari: रामः समुद्र वेलायां योगमास्थाय पौरुषम् । तत्याज लोकं मानुष्यं संयोज्यात्मानमात्मनि ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmaḥ samudra velāyāṃ yogamāsthāya pauruṣam tatyāja lokaṃ mānuṣyaṃ saṃyojyātmānamātmani

translation: Repairing to the sea-shore, Balarāma entered into Yogic meditation on the Supreme person and uniting his soul with the Supreme Soul, left this mortal world.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
The Mahābhārata differs in details (vide Mahābhārata Mausala. 4).
(1) When Kṛṣṇa saw his sons killed, he used all his divine weapons and ñnished with the Yādavas.
(2) Babhru and his charioteer stopped him telling him “most of Yādavas arc killed, let us find out where Balarāma is”. (Mahābhārata Mausala 2.43-47).
(3) Bala-Rāma was found resting under a tree. Kṛṣṇa deputed Dāruka to Arjuna to take away women and children from Dvārakā.
(4) When Babhru whom he deputed to protect women and property at Dvārakā was killed in Kṛṣṇa’s presence by a hunter’s arrow.
(5) Kṛṣṇa told Balarāma to wait till he returned from Dvārakā making arrangements for the protection of women and children.
(6) Kṛṣṇa went to Dvārakā and requested his father Vasudeva to protect women (and children) till the arrival of Arjuna at Dvārakā.
(7) He reported what happened at Prabhāsa and informed him of his decision to spend the rest of his life (along with Balarāma) in performing penance.
(8) On his return, he found Balarāma giving up his life by a Yogic process.
(9) Wandering in a pensive mood, he decided to give up his body and he lay in Yogic Samādhi when he was pierced with a hunter’s arrow.
Was it the second attempt on Kṛṣṇa’s life when the first-mis-aimed arrow killed Babhru instead of Kṛṣṇa?

devanagari: रामनिर्याणमालोक्य भगवान्देवकीसुतः । निषसाद धरोपस्थे तुष्णीमासाद्य पिप्पलम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmaniryāṇamālokya bhagavāndevakīsutaḥ niṣasāda dharopasthe tuṣṇīmāsādya pippalam
translation: Noticing the passing away of Balarāma, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, son of Devakī, quietly went to a Pippala tree and sat down on the ground (with his back resting against its Trunk) manifesting his (original) radiant four-armed personality dispelling the darkness of all the quarters with his brilliant lustre like a smokeless fire.

devanagari: बिभ्रच्चतुर्भुजं रूपं भ्राजिष्णु प्रभया स्वया । दिशो वितिमिराः कुर्वन्विधूम इव पावकः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bibhraccaturbhujaṃ rūpaṃ bhrājiṣṇu prabhayā svayā diśo vitimirāḥ kurvanvidhūma iva pāvakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Noticing the passing away of Balarāma, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, son of Devakī, quietly went to a Pippala tree and sat down on the ground (with his back resting against its Trunk) manifesting his (original) radiant four-armed personality dispelling the darkness of all the quarters with his brilliant lustre like a smokeless fire.

devanagari: श्रीवत्साङ्कं घनश्यामं तप्तहाटकवर्चसम् । कौशेयाम्बरयुग्मेन परिवीतं सुमङ्गलम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvatsāṅkaṃ ghanaśyāmaṃ taptahāṭakavarcasam kauśeyāmbarayugmena parivītaṃ sumaṅgalam
translation: (His form) was bearing the Śrīvatsa mark, blue like a rainy cloud, brilliant like burnished gold, wearing a pair of silken garments and the most auspicious (in appearance).

devanagari: सुन्दरस्मितवक्त्राब्जं नीलकुन्तलमण्डितम् । पुण्डरीकाभिरामाक्षं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: sundarasmitavaktrābjaṃ nīlakuntalamaṇḍitam puṇḍarīkābhirāmākṣaṃ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalam

translation: His lotus-like countenance was graced with charming smile and was adorned with bluish locks of hair and eyes beautiful like lotus; it was beautified with dazzling alligator- shaped ear-rings.

devanagari: कटिसूत्रब्रह्मसूत्र किरीटकटकाङ्गदैः । हारनूपुरमुद्राभिः कौस्तुभेन विराजितम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṭisūtrabrahmasūtra kirīṭakaṭakāṅgadaiḥ hāranūpuramudrābhiḥ kaustubhena virājitam
translation: It was adorned with a (brilliant) zone, sacred thread, a crown, bracelets, armlets, a necklace of pearls, (a pair of) anklets, rings and the Kaustubha jewel.

devanagari: वनमालापरीताङ्गं मूर्तिमद्भिर्निजायुधैः । कृत्वोरौ दक्षिणे पादमासीनं पङ्कजारुणम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vanamālāparītāṅgaṃ mūrtimadbhirnijāyudhaiḥ kṛtvorau dakṣiṇe pādamāsīnaṃ paṅkajāruṇam
translation: His person was encircled by a Vanamālā (a garland of special sylvan flowers). It was attended by his weapons in their personal forms. He was seated with his lotus-like reddish (left) foot on his right thigh.

devanagari: मुषलावशेषायःखण्ड कृतेषुर्लुब्धको जरा । मृगास्याकारं तच्चरणं विव्याध मृगशङ्कया ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: muṣalāvaśeṣāyaḥkhaṇḍa kṛteṣurlubdhako jarā mṛgāsyākāraṃ taccaraṇaṃ vivyādha mṛgaśaṅkayā

translation: A hunter called ‘Jarā’ who fixed on the point of his arrow the un-pulverized remnant of the iron pestle (“born” from Sāmba[7]) pierced therewith his foot which appeared in shape like the mouth of a deer (wrongly presuming him to be a deer).

devanagari: चतुर्भुजं तं पुरुषं दृष्ट्वा स कृतकिल्बिषः । भीतः पपात शिरसा पादयोरसुरद्विषः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: caturbhujaṃ taṃ puruṣaṃ dṛṣṭvā sa kṛtakilbiṣaḥ bhītaḥ papāta śirasā pādayorasuradviṣaḥ

translation: Seeing that it was a four-armed person (whom he hit), the horrified perpetrator of the (homicidal) sin prostrated himself at the feet of Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of Asuras, touching his feet with his head.

devanagari: अजानता कृतमिदं पापेन मधुसूदन । क्षन्तुमर्हसि पापस्य उत्तमःश्लोक मेऽनघ ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ajānatā kṛtamidaṃ pāpena madhusūdana kṣantumarhasi pāpasya uttamaḥśloka me

translation: ‘It is sheedy out of ignorance that this has been committed by me, O Madhusūdana. It behoves you, O faultless Lord of hallowing glory, to pardon me for this sin.

devanagari: यस्यानुस्मरणं नृणामज्ञानध्वान्तनाशनम् । वदन्ति तस्य ते विष्णो मयासाधु कृतं प्रभो ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyānusmaraṇaṃ nṛṇāmajñānadhvāntanāśanam vadanti tasya te viṣṇo mayāsādhu kṛtaṃ prabho

translation: This sinful act against you has been committed by me, O Lord Viṣṇu, whose constant remembrance, the wise say is efficacious enough to destroy the ignorance of men.

devanagari: तन्माऽशु जहि वैकुण्ठ पाप्मानं मृगलुब्धकम् । यथा पुनरहं त्वेवं न कुर्यां सदतिक्रमम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tanmā yathā punarahaṃ tvevaṃ na kuryāṃ sadatikramam
translation: Therefore, put an end to my life immediately, O Vaikuṇṭha (Viṣṇu) so that I should not commit such an offence against any other righteous person.

devanagari: यस्यात्मयोगरचितं न विदुर्विरिञ्चो । रुद्रादयोऽस्य तनयाः पतयो गिरां ये । त्वन्मायया पिहितदृष्टय एतदञ्जः । किं तस्य ते वयमसद्गतयो गृणीमः...
english_devnagari: yasyātmayogaracitaṃ na vidurviriñco rudrādayo tvanmāyayā pihitadṛṣṭaya etadañjaḥ kiṃ tasya te vayamasadgatayo gṛṇīmaḥ
translation: (If you agree that my shooting an arrow at you is not my fault but the effect of the Brahmanical curse, I submit) even god Brahmā, his sons, god-like Rudra and others, and the Vedic seers (the masters of the divine words) have failed to grasp the working of your Yoga Māyā. How can we of impious birth whose vision has been blind-folded by your Māyā can say anything about this sportive act (līlā) of yours? (Be gracious enough to kill me and absolve me of my sins).’

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मा भैर्जरे त्वमुत्तिष्ठ काम एष कृतो हि मे । याहि त्वं मदनुज्ञातः स्वर्गं सुकृतिनां पदम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca mā bhairjare tvamuttiṣṭha kāma eṣa kṛto hi me yāhi tvaṃ madanujñātaḥ svargaṃ sukṛtināṃ padam

translation:
The Lord replied:
‘Be not afraid, O Jarā, Get up. What you have done is exactly what was willed by me. (Cast off your body and) with my permission, do you go to heaven, the region of the righteous people.’

devanagari: इत्यादिष्टो भगवता कृष्णेनेच्छाशरीरिणा । त्रिः परिक्रम्य तं नत्वा विमानेन दिवं ययौ ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ityādiṣṭo bhagavatā kṛṣṇenecchāśarīriṇā triḥ parikramya taṃ natvā vimānena divaṃ yayau

translation: Thus commanded by the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa who takes any form at his will[8], the hunter circumambulated (by the right) Lord Kṛṣṇa, bowed to him and went to heaven in a celestial car.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Lord’s form, constituted of pure Sattva, disappeared. His apparent form was only an outward semblance of his real form—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: दारुकः कृष्णपदवीमन्विच्छन्नधिगम्य ताम् । वायुं तुलसिकामोदमाघ्रायाभिमुखं ययौ ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: dārukaḥ kṛṣṇapadavīmanvicchannadhigamya tām vāyuṃ tulasikāmodamāghrāyābhimukhaṃ yayau

translation: Dāruka was tracing Kṛṣṇa by his foot-prints, came in search of him. He identified the spot by the scent of the Tulasī borne on the wind and he stood before him.

devanagari: तं तत्र तिग्मद्युभिरायुधैर्वृतं । ह्यश्वत्थमूले कृतकेतनं पतिम् । स्नेहप्लुतात्मा निपपात पादयो । रथादवप्लुत्य सबाष्पलोचनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tatra tigmadyubhirāyudhairvṛtaṃ hyaśvatthamūle kṛtaketanaṃ patim snehaplutātmā nipapāta pādayo rathādavaplutya sabāṣpalocanaḥ

translation: He saw his Lord there seated at the foot of the Aśvattha tree, attended upon by his sharp brilliant weapons in person. With his eyes bedimmed with tears and heart overflowing with loyalty and affection, he jumped down from (the charioteer’s seat in) the chariot and immediately fell at his feet.

devanagari: अपश्यतस्त्वच्चरणाम्बुजं प्रभो दृष्टिः प्रणष्टा तमसि प्रविष्टा । दिशो न जाने न लभे च शान्तिं यथा निशायामुडुपे प्रणष्टे ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: apaśyatastvaccaraṇāmbujaṃ prabho dṛṣṭiḥ praṇaṣṭā tamasi praviṣṭā diśo na jāne na labhe ca śāntiṃ yathā niśāyāmuḍupe praṇaṣṭe
translation: (Dāruka said:) O Lord, just as one feels lost in darkness, at night, after the setting of the moon, ever since I did not see your lotus-feet, my vision was enveloped in darkness, and was completely lost. I do not see directions (how to behave as well). I have lost the peace of my mind.”

devanagari: इति ब्रुवति सूते वै रथो गरुडलाञ्छनः । खमुत्पपात राजेन्द्र साश्वध्वज उदीक्षतः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti bruvati sūte vai ratho garuḍalāñchanaḥ khamutpapāta rājendra sāśvadhvaja udīkṣataḥ

translation: Even as the charioteer (Dāruka) was speaking thus, his chariot with its flag bearing the ensign of Garuḍa, soared up into heaven along with horses, while Dāruka merely looked on (agape).

devanagari: तमन्वगच्छन्दिव्यानि विष्णुप्रहरणानि च । तेनातिविस्मितात्मानं सूतमाह जनार्दनः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tamanvagacchandivyāni viṣṇupraharaṇāni ca tenātivismitātmānaṃ sūtamāha janārdanaḥ

translation: Even the celestial weapons of Viṣṇu followed the chariot. Lord Kṛṣṇa then addressed his charioteer who was dazed at heart with utter astonishment (at the happening).

devanagari: गच्छ द्वारवतीं सूत ज्ञातीनां निधनं मिथः । सङ्कर्षणस्य निर्याणं बन्धुभ्यो ब्रूहि मद्दशाम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: gaccha dvāravatīṃ sūta jñātīnāṃ nidhanaṃ mithaḥ saṅkarṣaṇasya niryāṇaṃ bandhubhyo brūhi maddaśām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: You go to Dvārakā, O charioteer, and report to the Yādava kinsmen thereof the internecine conflict and mutual slaughter of our clansmen, Balarāma’s passing away and my present condition.

devanagari: द्वारकायां च न स्थेयं भवद्भिश्च स्वबन्धुभिः । मया त्यक्तां यदुपुरीं समुद्रः प्लावयिष्यति ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: dvārakāyāṃ ca na stheyaṃ bhavadbhiśca svabandhubhiḥ mayā tyaktāṃ yadupurīṃ samudraḥ plāvayiṣyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: Warn them that the sea will submerge the city of Yādavas (Dvārakā) abandoned by me. Hence you (i.e. both Dāruka and the remaining inhabitants of Dvārakā) alongwith their kinsmen should not stay at Dvārakā.

devanagari: स्वं स्वं परिग्रहं सर्वे आदाय पितरौ च नः । अर्जुनेनाविताः सर्व इन्द्र प्रस्थं गमिष्यथ ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: svaṃ svaṃ parigrahaṃ sarve ādāya pitarau ca naḥ arjunenāvitāḥ sarva indra prasthaṃ gamiṣyatha
translation: Taking with you the members of your families at well as our parents, all of you go to Indraprastha, under the escort of Arjuna, on the way.

devanagari: त्वं तु मद्धर्ममास्थाय ज्ञाननिष्ठ उपेक्षकः । मन्मायारचितामेतां विज्ञयोपशमं व्रज ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ tu maddharmamāsthāya jñānaniṣṭha upekṣakaḥ manmāyāracitāmetāṃ vijñayopaśamaṃ vraja
translation: Establishing yourself firmly in spiritual wisdom and remaining indifferent (to weal and woe) you follow the spiritual path proclaimed by me. Realizing that all this universe or Saṃsāra is nothing but the creation of Māyā (and hence unreal) you should attain spiritual peace of mind.

devanagari: इत्युक्तस्तं परिक्रम्य नमस्कृत्य पुनः पुनः । तत्पादौ शीर्ष्ण्युपाधाय दुर्मनाः प्रययौ पुरीम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktastaṃ parikramya namaskṛtya punaḥ punaḥ tatpādau śīrṣṇyupādhāya durmanāḥ prayayau purīm
translation:
Thus commanded Dāruka went round Lord Kṛṣṇa (by the right-hand side) and bowing him down again and again and placing his feet on his head (i.e. touching them with his head), he went to the city (of Dvārakā) with a heavy heart.
